title: Learning from Home
passage:
Learning from Home

Paragraph A
Online learning is a popular way to study today. Students do not go to a traditional school building. Instead, they use a computer, a tablet, or a smartphone connected to the internet. They can study from their bedroom, a library, or a coffee shop. Many schools and universities now offer online classes for students of all ages.

Paragraph B
Sarah is a 15-year-old student from London. She does online school every day. Her day starts at 9:00 AM. She turns on her laptop and watches live lessons with her teachers. Sarah likes online learning because she can work at her own speed. If she does not understand a math lesson, she can watch the video again. She also has more free time to play the piano and help her parents in the garden.

Paragraph C
David is a 30-year-old businessman from New York. He works in an office from Monday to Friday. In the evening, David studies Spanish online. He wants to speak Spanish for his job. He does not have time to go to a real language school. With online learning, David can study at 10:00 PM when his children are asleep. He uses a special mobile application to practice speaking with a teacher from Spain.

Paragraph D
However, online learning can also be difficult. Some students feel lonely because they do not see their friends. Sometimes, the internet connection is slow, and the video stops. Students must also be very organized. There is no teacher in the room to tell them what to do. But for many people, online learning is the best way to get an education.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: According to Paragraph A, online students can study from...

options.C: their bedroom, a library, or a coffee shop

questions.explanation: Paragraph A states: 'They can study from their bedroom, a library, or a coffee shop.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What time does Sarah's online school start?
questions.explanation: Paragraph B states: 'Her day starts at 9:00 AM.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why does David study Spanish online?
questions.explanation: Paragraph C states: 'He wants to speak Spanish for his job.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.
questions.text: Sarah can watch her math lessons again if she does not understand them.
questions.explanation: Paragraph B states: 'If she does not understand a math lesson, she can watch the video again.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.
questions.text: David works in an office on the weekend.
questions.explanation: Paragraph C states: 'He works in an office from Monday to Friday.' This means he does not work in the office on the weekend.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.
questions.text: David's Spanish teacher lives in Madrid.
questions.explanation: Paragraph C says the teacher is 'from Spain', but it does not mention if the teacher lives in Madrid.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.
questions.text: Online learning is sometimes difficult because of slow internet.
questions.explanation: Paragraph D states: 'Sometimes, the internet connection is slow, and the video stops.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: To study online, students need a computer, a tablet, or a ______.
questions.explanation: Paragraph A states: '...they use a computer, a tablet, or a smartphone connected to the internet.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: In her free time, Sarah likes to play the ______.
questions.explanation: Paragraph B states: 'She also has more free time to play the piano...'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: David uses a special ______ to practice speaking Spanish.
questions.explanation: Paragraph C states: 'He uses a special mobile application to practice speaking...'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Some students feel ______ because they do not see their friends.
questions.explanation: Paragraph D states: 'Some students feel lonely because they do not see their friends.'

vocabulary.meaning: following ideas or methods that have existed for a long time
a small computer that you can carry easily
sad because you are not with other people
planning things carefully and keeping things in order
a computer program designed for a particular purpose (app)

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (A1-A2)

1. Kỹ thuật tìm từ khóa (Scanning): Hãy chú ý tìm các danh từ riêng như Sarah, David, hoặc các con số như 9:00 AM, 30-year-old để tìm thông tin nhanh chóng.

2. Điền từ vào chỗ trống: Đảm bảo từ bạn viết giống hệt từ xuất hiện trong bài đọc. Không thay đổi dạng của từ (ví dụ: giữ nguyên số ít/số nhiều).

3. True/False/Not Given: Nếu bài viết không đề cập chi tiết cụ thể (như thành phố Madrid), hãy chọn NOT GIVEN thay vì tự suy luận.
